concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains meaningful images, specifically the official North Carolina state seal in the header and the DEQ logo in the footer. While the HTML output contains elements for these images, the provided image manifest indicates that all extracted images have 'valid: false' due to an extraction error ('No module named PIL'). Therefore, the base64 data is corrupt, and the images will be broken when rendered in a browser.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Official North Carolina state seal at the top center of the page, and the DEQ logo at the bottom left.
concerns.output_quote: Image manifest reports: "valid": false, "error": "No module named 'PIL'" for all three extracted images.

concerns.description: Scanned document: source has 0 extractable text but output contains 322 words (OCR'd from images)
concerns.analysis: baseline_words=0, gemini_words=322. Entire document is image-based; output likely duplicates content as both page image and extracted text.

concerns.description: Output has more images than source PDF (ratio 3.0x) — possible image duplication
concerns.analysis: image_count_ratio=3.00. More images in output than in source indicates duplicated or fabricated image elements.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.
The source document contains an underlined sentence in the third body paragraph to draw attention to an administrative instruction. This underline emphasis is completely lost in the output JSON.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Please attach this letter to the cover of your existing mining permit for future reference. (underlined in source)
concerns.output_quote: Please attach this letter to the cover of your existing mining permit for future reference.
concerns.location: Third paragraph of the letter body
